title: 第 23 節
en: total of verses as 10,402 ; but here again, if we cast up the number of verses in each metre, according to his own statement, we get 10,409 instead of 10,402. These differences are startling if we consider the general accuracy of the exegetical works of the Brahmans; but they may arise either from faults in the MSS. of the Anukramanis, or from the fact that some of the Khilas were included, though, according to their own professions, both Saunaka and Katyayana would seem to exclude these later hymns from their Anukramanis. The following table will show the distribution of metres according to Saunaka : —

G&yatri Brought forward Ushnih Ashti Anushtubh - Atyashti - Brihati Dhriti Pankti Atidhriti - Trishtubh - Ekapada - Jagati Dvipada - Atijagati Pragatha Barhata 194 Sakvari Kakubha - Atisakvari - Mahabarhata Carried forward For the Yajur-veda we have three Anukramanis, one for the Atreyi-sakha of the Taittiriyas, the other for the Sakha of the Charayaniyas, the third for the Madhyandina-sakh& of the Vajasaneyins. The former x differs from other Anukramanis in so far as it contains an index not of the Sanhita only, but 1 MS. E. I. H. 1623, 965.

ANUKRAMANIS. 223 also of the Brahmana and the Aranyaka. Its object is not simply to enumerate the Kandas (Ashtakas), Prasnas, Anuvakas, and Kandikas as they follow in the text, but rather to indicate the chief subjects of this Yeda, and to bring together the different passages where the same sacrifice with its supplements is treated. Though we do not possess a MS. of the Atreyi-sakha, it is possible to identify nearly the whole of the Index with the text of the Sanhita l, the Brahmana 2, and the Aranyaka 3 which we possess. The Atreyi-sakha, though not mentioned in the Charanavyuha, must be considered as a subdivision of the Aukhiya-sakha ; and the Anukramani says that Vaisampayana handed it down to Yaska Paingi, Y&ska to Tittiri, Tittiri to Ukha, and Ukha to Atreya, who was the author of a Pada-text4, while Kundina composed a commentary (vritti) on the same Sakha. The Apastamba-sakha, of which we possess the complete Brahmana, is a subdivision of the Khandikeyas.

There is a curious tradition, preserved in the Ivandanukrama, that, although the greater portion of the Atreyi-sakha was originally taught by Tittiri, some chapters of it owed their origin to Katha, the founder of the Kathaka-sakha. This assertion is confirmed by Sayana in his Commentary on the Taittiriy aranyaka. The chapters ascribed to Katha and called the Kathakam, are found at the end of the Brahmana and the beginning of the Aranyaka. They contain 1 MS. E. I. H. 1701, 1702; name of Sakha unknown.
2 MS. E. I. H. 293, containing the three books of the Apastamba-brahmana.
1. The Savitragnichayana with the Brahmana, Tait.-brahm. iii. 10.
6. Yaisvasrijachiti. Tait.-br. iii. 12. 6 — 9, end of Brahmana.

They are given here as they follow one another in the text of the Apastamba-Sakha, and this order is confirmed in every particular by Say ana's Commentary (MS. E. I. H. 1145), which is in fact a commentary intended for the Apastamba-sakha of the Taittiriyabrahmana. According to his introductory remarks prefixed to each Anuvaka, the Savitrachiti occupies the tenth, the Nachiketachiti the eleventh Prapathaka. In the twelfth Prapathaka, he remarks, the Chaturhotra and Yaisvasrija should be explained. But as the ishtis, called the Divahsyenis and Apadyas, form part of the complete Chaturhotra (they stand either in the middle or at the end of it), they are explained first. Thus we find in the beginning of the twelfth Prapathaka (iii. 12. 1.), the pratikas of the Yajyanuvakyas of the Divahsyenis ; in iii. 12.2. the rules for the same ishtis ; and in the same manner, the Yajyanuvakyas of the Apadyas in iii. 12. 3., and the rules in iii. 12. 4. Then follows the Chaturhotra-chayana in iii. 12. 5 , and in the last four Anuvakas the Yaisvasrijachayana, A different order seems to have been observed in the Atreyi-sakha of the Taittiriya-brahmana, for, although the same chapters are here ascribed to ANUKRAMANis. 225 Katha, their arrangement must have differed, unless we suppose that the author of the Kandanukrama introduced an alteration. He writes : " Tavat Tittirih provacha. (Tittiris Taittiriyasakhapravartako 'nyebhyo munibhyah sishyebhyah provacha.) Athashtau Kathakani (athanantaram KathakasakMpravartakena Kathakamunina proktany uchyante) :

8. Svadhy&ya-br&hmana, Taittiriya-aranyaka, i. 2." The second Anukramani of the Yajur-veda which we possess, belongs to the Charayaniya-sakha, and is called the Mantrarshadhyaya.1 The only copy which we have of it is found in the same MS. which contains the Charaka-sakha,2and it is evidently intended as an index to this sakha. Nor is there anything anomalous in this, if we remember that the Charayaniya-sakh& is a subdivision of the Charaka-sakha. But what is less intelligible is the title given to the text, which instead of Yajur-veda, is called in the MS. Yajur-veda-kathaka. This title, Kathaka, cannot well refer to the sakha of the Kathas, for this is itself a subdivision of the Charakas. It must most likely be taken in the same sense in which Kathaka was explained before, L e. " Kathakamunina proktam ; " though it is strange that the very chapters which in theApastamba- 1 See Catalogue of the Berlin MSS., No. 142.
2 The title is " Ekottarasatadhvaryusakhaprabhedabhinne Yaj urvedakat hake Charakasakha."

sakha of the Taittiriyaka are ascribed to Katha, are wanting in our Sakha, while all the other sacrifices which are described in the Taittiriya-sanhita and Brahmana, are laid down in very much the same order.

The third Anukramani, that of the Madhyandinasakha of the Vajasaneyaka, is ascribed to Katyayana, who is mentioned also as the author of an Anuvakanukramani. It gives the names of the poets, the deities, and the metres, for all the verses of the Sanhita, including the Khila (Adhyaya 26-35.) and the Sukriya portions. (Adhy. 36-40.)

For the Sama-veda we have two classes of Anukramanis, the former more ancient, the latter more modern than those of the other Vedas which we have hitherto examined. One index to the hymns of the Sama-veda (following the order of the Veyagana and Aranyagana) has been preserved under the name of Arsheya-brahmana l, a title by which this work is admitted within the pale of the revealed literature of the Brahmans. Allusions to the names of poets and deities of different hymns occur in the Brahmanas of other Yedas also ; but in none, except the Sama-veda, have these scattered observations been arranged into regular Anukramanis before the beginning of the Sutra period, or been incorporated in the body of their revealed literature. What the Brahmans call Sruti or revelation, signifies, as we saw, what is more ancient than the Sutras ; and that the Arsheya-brahmana is earlier at least than Katyayana, can be proved by the fact of Katyayana's quoting passages from it.2 It has been pointed out as a distinguishing mark of the Brah- 1 See Benfey, Saina-veda, p. vii.
zh: 總詩頌數為 10,402 首；但在此，如果我們根據他自己的說法逐一計算各種格律的詩頌數，得到的是 10,409 首而非 10,402 首。考慮到婆羅門人釋義著作的普遍準確性，這些差異令人震驚；但這些差異可能源自《詩頌目錄》（Anukramanis）的抄本錯誤，或者源自某些《補編讚歌》（Khilas）被包括在內的事實——儘管根據他們自己的宣稱，桑耶迦（Saunaka）和迦頻延那（Katyayana）似乎都會將這些後來的讚歌排除在《詩頌目錄》之外。下表將顯示根據桑耶迦的格律分佈情況：—

蓋亞特里（Gayatri）烏什尼赫（Ushnih）阿什提（Ashti）阿努什圖卜（Anushtubh）─ 阿提亞什提（Atyashti）─ 布里哈提（Brihati）德里提（Dhriti）潘克提（Pankti）阿提德里提（Atidhriti）─ 特里什圖卜（Trishtubh）─ 埃卡帕達（Ekapada）─ 雅加提（Jagati）德維帕達（Dvipada）─ 阿提雅加提（Atijagati）普拉加塔（Pragatha）巴爾哈塔（Barhata）194 薩克瓦里（Sakvari）卡庫巴（Kakubha）─ 阿提薩克瓦里（Atisakvari）─ 摩訶巴爾哈塔（Mahabarhata）遞前 若干耶柱吠陀（Yajur-veda），我們擁有三部《頌讚索引》（Anukramanis），其一乃塔衣提里亞學派（Atreyi-sakha of the Taittiriyas）者，其二乃車羅耶尼亞學派（Sakha of the Charayaniyas）者，其三乃瓦賈桑涅因學派之中晝派（Madhyandina-sakh& of the Vajasaneyins）者。前者x與其他《頌讚索引》之差異在於，它所含之索引不僅止於《本集》（Sanhita）而已，1 手稿 E. I. H. 1623, 965。

**ANUKRAMANIS。223** 亦為婆羅門書（Brahmana）與森林書（Aranyaka）之索引。其目的不僅在於單純列舉吠陀中依序出現的康達（Kandas）、八體（Ashtakas）、普若奏那（Prasnas）、阿努瓦卡（Anuvakas）及坎迪卡（Kandikas），更在於指示此吠陀的主要主題，並匯集論述同一祭祀及其補充部分的不同段落。雖然我們不擁有阿特雷伊‧沙卡（Atreyi-sakha）的手稿，但仍有可能將幾乎整個索引與我們所擁有的三部文獻相互比對：梨俱吠陀本集（Sanhita）1、婆羅門書2 及森林書3。阿特雷伊‧沙卡雖未在《儀軌綱要》（Charanavyuha）中提及，但應被視為奧克希亞‧沙卡（Aukhiya-sakha）的一個分支；《阿奴克拉瑪尼》（Anukramani）記載韋薩濕帕延（Vaisampayana）將此傳給雅斯卡‧派英吉（Yaska Paingi），雅斯卡傳給提提里（Tittiri），提提里傳給烏卡（Ukha），烏卡傳給阿特雷耶（Atreya），後者著有《詞文註疏》（Pada-text）4，而昆迪那（Kundina）為同一沙卡著成了註疏（vritti）。阿帕斯坦巴‧沙卡（Apastamba-sakha）為康迪克耶族（Khandikeyas）的一個分支，我們擁有其完整的婆羅門書。

在《因旺達努克拉瑪》（Ivandanukrama）中保存著一個有趣的傳統，述及雖然阿特雷耶分派（Atreyi-sakha）的大部分內容原本由蒂蒂里（Tittiri）傳授，但其中某些章節的起源卻歸因於卡塔（Katha），即卡塔卡分派（Kathaka-sakha）的創立者。這一主張得到了沙亞那（Sayana）在其《泰蒂利亞《森林書》》（Taittiriy aranyaka）註疏中的確認。歸屬於卡塔並被稱為《卡塔卡》（Kathakam）的章節，位於《梵書》（Brahmana）的末尾與《森林書》（Aranyaka）的開始處。它們包含¹ 手稿 E. I. H. 1701、1702；分派名稱不詳。
2 MS. E. I. H. 293，收錄阿帕斯坦巴梵書（Apastamba-brahmana）三卷。
見 MS. Bodl. Wilson, 361。
224 阿奴克拉曼尼（*Anukkramaṇi*）。
《讚美日神經》(Savitragnichayana)附《梵書》，《泰帝利耶梵書》(Tait.-brahm.) 第三卷第十章。
2. 《納奇凱塔查耶那篇》(Nachiketachayana)，《泰迪利亞梵書》(Tait.-br.) iii. 11。
3. Divahsyenaya ishtayah. Tait.-br. iii. 12.
4. Apadya ishtayah. Tait.-br. iii. 12. 3 & 4.
5. Chaturhotrachiti. Tait.-br. iii. 12. 5.
6. Yaisvasrijachiti. Tait.-br. iii. 12. 6 — 9，梵書末尾。
7. 阿魯那刻圖迦奇地。《泰帝利亞林住部》第一卷第一節。
8《自學梵書》（*Svadhyaya-brahmana*）。《泰帝利亞林書》第一卷第二章（*Tait.-aranyaka, i. 2*）。

這些內容在阿帕斯坦巴分支（Apastamba-Sakha）的經文中依次排列，並且這個順序在每一點都得到了沙耶那註釋書（MS. E. I. H. 1145）的確認。該註釋書實際上是為泰帝利耶婆羅門經（Taittiriyabrahmana）的阿帕斯坦巴分支（Apastamba-sakha）而作的註釋。根據他為每一阿努瓦迦（Anuvaka）所附的導言性論述，薩毘特拉智慧（Savitrachiti）占據第十章，納奇克塔智慧（Nachiketachiti）占據第十一章。在第十二章中，他指出，四祭主法（Chaturhotra）和耶伊什瓦斯利傑法（Yaisvasrija）應當加以解說。但由於稱為天界祭祀（Divahsyenis）和阿帕迪耶祭祀（Apadyas）的伊什提（ishtis）構成完整四祭主法（Chaturhotra）的一部分（它們要嘛位於其中間，要嘛位於其末端），所以首先對它們進行解說。因此，我們在第十二章的開頭（iii. 12. 1.）找到了天界祭祀的雅吉亞努瓦迦（Yajyanuvakyas）的護持經句（pratikas）；在 iii. 12.2. 中找到了相同伊什提的規則；同樣地，在 iii. 12. 3. 中找到了阿帕迪耶祭祀的雅吉亞努瓦迦，規則在 iii. 12. 4. 中；接著是 iii. 12. 5 中的四祭主法選集（Chaturhotra-chayana），最後四個阿努瓦迦中是耶伊什瓦斯利傑選集（Yaisvasrijachayana）。在泰帝利耶婆羅門經的阿特瑞伊分支（Atreyi-sakha）中似乎奉行了不同的順序，因為儘管這些相同的章節在此被歸屬於阿努克拉馬尼（ANUKRAMANI. 225）和喀他（Katha），但它們的編排必定是有所不同的，除非我們假定《坎達努克拉瑪》（Kandanukrama）的作者引入了改變。他寫道：「那時提帝利斯說（提帝利斯是提帝利耶分支的開創者，對其他仙人弟子如此說）。於是八部喀他經（隨後由喀他分支的開創者喀他仙人所述説的稱為喀他經）：
薩維特拉（Savitra），《泰帝利亞梵書》（Taittiriya-brahmana），第三卷，第十章。
2. Mchiketa 《歌者奧義書》（Kena Upanishad）第三篇第十一節
3. Chaturhotra „ „ iii. 12. 5.
4. Yaisvasrija《梨俱吠陀》iii. 12. 6—9。
5. 阿魯那，《泰帝利耶林書》（Taittiriya-aranyaka），第一卷第一部。
6. Divahsyenis，《泰帝利耶梵書》（Taittiriya-brahmana），iii. 12. 1—2。
7. 阿帕迪亞斯（Apadyas）《吠陀》iii. 12. 3—4。

8. Svadhy&ya-br&hmana, Taittiriya-aranyaka, i. 2." 我們所擁有的夜柔吠陀（Yajur-veda）第二部《序列書》（Anukramani）屬於《遮羅延派》（Charayaniya-sakha），名稱為《咒文六章》（Mantrarshadhyaya）。¹ 我們僅有的這部著作抄本見於同一份手稿，該手稿亦包含《遮羅迦派》（Charaka-sakha），²顯然是作為該派的索引而編纂。若我們記得《遮羅延派》（Charayaniya-sakha）本身是《遮羅迦派》（Charaka-sakha）的分支，則此事並無異常之處。但較難理解的是該文本所冠之標題，在手稿中，此標題並非稱為夜柔吠陀（Yajur-veda），而是稱為《夜柔吠陀‧卡塔卡》（Yajur-veda-kathaka）。此標題「卡塔卡」（Kathaka）實難指稱卡塔派（Kathas）的派系，因為卡塔派本身亦是遮羅迦派的分支。最可能的解釋是採取先前對「卡塔卡」所作的同一義項，即「由卡塔卡聖仙所述」（Kathakamunina proktam）；¹ 惟有一點殊為奇異，即在阿帕斯坦巴派（Apastamba） ¹ 見《柏林手稿目錄》（Catalogue of the Berlin MSS.），第 142 號。
2 標題為「《夜柔吠陀》迦羅迦分支之超過百種祭祀書區分本」(Ekottarasatadhvaryusakhaprabhedabhinne Yajurvedakathake Charakasakha)。
226 阿奴克拉曼
歸屬於迦膩（Katha）的《泰鐵利耶書》（Taittiriyaka）各分家中的一些祭祀儀式，在我們的分家中是缺失的，而所有其他在《泰鐵利耶梵書彙編》（Taittiriya-sanhita）和《梵書》（Brahmana）中所記述的祭祀，在我們的分家中幾乎都按同樣的順序規定了下來。

第三部《經文索引》（Anukramani）是《瓦亞薩內亞迦》（Vajasaneyaka）的《中夜林迦派》（Madhyandinasakha）版本，傳統歸於迦多衍那（Katyayana）所作，他同時也被提及為《分經索引》（Anuvakanukramani）的編著者。該索引列舉了《誦本》（Sanhita）全部詩句的詩人名、神祇名與韻律，包括《補編》（Khila，第26-35章）與《美言篇》（Sukriya）部分（第36-40章）。

關於《同一吠陀》（Sama-veda），我們有兩類《經索引》（Anukramanis），前者比我們迄今所審視的其他吠陀的經索引更為古老，後者則較為近代。其中保存於《詩人梵書》（Arsheya-brahmana）名下的一部《同一吠陀》讚歌索引（依循《韋亞迦納》與《阿蘭亞迦納》的順序），被公認納入梵志階層的啟示文獻範圍內。其他吠陀的梵書中亦出現對不同讚歌的詩人名諱與神祇名號的典故；然而，唯獨在《同一吠陀》中，這些零散的觀察才在《蘇特拉》時期肇始之前被整編成規則的《經索引》，或併入其啟示文獻的本體。梵志階層所謂的《斯魯提》（Sruti）或啟示，如我們所見，係指比《蘇特拉》更古老的內容；而《詩人梵書》至少早於迦多耶那（Katyayana），此點可由迦多耶那引述該書段落的事實而得以證明。¹ 這已被指出為梵志階層的一個區別性標誌¹。 --- ¹ 見班費（Benfey），《同一吠陀研究》，第七頁。
